Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Intermediate A sophisticated cyber attack targeting a transportation system's infrastructure, compromising critical operations through a multi-stage attack involving exploitation of vulnerabilities, deployment of malicious payloads, and persistence mechanisms leading to operational disruption.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2020-0938 (Suggested): Microsoft Windows SMBv2 vulnerability allowing remote code execution.
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 384 - Application API Message Manipulation via Man-in-the-Middle
stage.description: The attackers exploited a known vulnerability in an exposed FTP server with weak credentials, gaining unauthorized access to the transportation system's network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2023-36563 (Suggested): Vulnerability in system updates allowing arbitrary code execution.
stage.description: A custom payload was deployed to disrupt traffic control operations, targeting critical system components for immediate impact.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: A new scheduled task was created to maintain persistent access across system reboots, ensuring continuous presence within the network.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: An existing service was modified to run with elevated privileges, enabling further unauthorized actions within the system.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: A rootkit was installed to hide malicious activities, evading detection by antivirus and endpoint protection solutions.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attack disrupted traffic light operations, causing gridlock and potential safety hazards in urban transportation networks.

Scenario.summary: This scenario outlines a multi-stage cyber attack against a transportation system, leveraging vulnerabilities and persistence mechanisms to achieve long-term access and significant operational disruption. The attack highlights the importance of securing internet-facing assets and implementing robust defense mechanisms to mitigate such threats.
